final_answer:
The river that flows through Paris, France, is the Seine. According to the information provided, the Seine is approximately 483 miles (780 kilometers) long. 

Therefore, the name of the river is the Seine, and its approximate length is 483 miles (780 kilometers).

Title: Seine - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seine
Snippet: The Seine (/ seɪn, sɛn / sayn, sen; [1] French: [sɛn] ⓘ) is a 777-kilometre-long (483 mi) river in northern France. [2] Its drainage basin is in the Paris Basin (a geological relative lowland) covering most of northern France. [3] It rises at Source-Seine, 30 kilometres (19 mi) northwest of Dijon in northeastern France in the Langres plateau, flowing through Paris and into the English ...

Snippet: Seine River, river of France, after the Loire its longest. It rises 18 miles (30 kilometres) northwest of Dijon and flows in a northwesterly direction through Paris before emptying into the English Channel at Le Havre. The river is 485 miles (780 kilometres) long and with its tributaries drains an
---
Title: All about the Seine River - Paris' Most Beautiful Avenue
URL: https://worldinparis.com/seine-river-paris
Snippet: The Seine River is Paris' most beautiful avenue and one of the main reasons to visit Paris. The River Seine in Paris winds its way through the heart of the city, bisecting it from east to west. One of the most famous French Rivers, the Seine River, is the origin of Paris, and the city's development is uniquely tied to the River.
---
Title: Which River Runs Through Paris? - WorldAtlas
URL: https://www.worldatlas.com/articles/which-river-runs-through-paris.html
Snippet: Which River Runs Through Paris? Paris is on the River Seine. Paris is the largest city and the capital city of France. Paris is on the River Seine, or the Seine River. The Seine River is 483 miles long and gets its water from various tributaries including Aube, the Loing, the Yonne, the Eure, the Essonne, the Aisne, and the Marne rivers.

It includes Notre-Dame Cathedral, the Louvre Museum, the Eiffel Tower, Hotel de Ville (Paris City Hall), the Trocadéro (Palais de Chaillot), Musée d'Orsay and all bridges. Click on map to enlarge and print it.
